next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 10

Under the kitchen light, the milk was going to be a problem. The next evening, Yor knew only that the milk was going to be a problem.

She held the carton at an angle and read the date stamp. Tomorrow. Back into the refrigerator it went, behind the leftover stew, where Loid was less likely to discover it. He had opinions about expiration dates that she considered disproportionate to the actual science of dairy spoilage, but she'd never found a way to say that without sounding like she was defending spoiled milk, which she wasn't. What she was defending was the principle that milk didn't become poison at midnight on the printed date. She was fairly sure about this.

Bond had taken over the middle of the living room rug again, chin pressed into the worn border, sleeping with the calm certainty that everyone else would step around him. His breathing came deep and even, with a faint whistle at the end of each exhale, and one back leg twitched with whatever passed through a precognitive dog's sleeping mind. Yor sometimes caught Anya watching Bond with an expression that suggested she was listening to something Yor couldn't hear, but the habit was so old and so gentle that Yor had filed it years ago among the things about her daughter that she loved without understanding.

Across the hall, Anya's old bedroom door stood half open. The bedspread was smooth. At noon a message had come from an embassy number Yor didn't recognize: *paperwork ran long don't wait up love you*. Too neat. Too bright at the end. Yor had answered with *Eat something hot if you can*, then, beca...
open_hooks_to_carry: Assassination attempt: two military-trained assailants, real violence, real injuries
Anya's combat training visible — she is her parents' daughter
The attack may connect to the mole — intelligence chains that touch Anya's false report
Safe house aftermath: wound care, adrenaline drop, proximity
Childhood memories and genuine laughter leading to a kiss
Anya's inability to stop reading Damian's thoughts during intimacy — the moral weight of unearned access
Post-kiss: Damian's thoughts about Anya Forger specifically nearly surface at full resolution — the name is right there

payoffs_to_land.description: The guilt of reading Damian's unguarded feelings during an intimate moment crystallizes the moral asymmetry seeded in Ch 3
The recognition is now imminent — Damian is thinking of Anya Forger by name, though he hasn't committed to the identification yet

prev_tail_excerpt:
...racked watch Yor had given him years ago and thought, with deep private irritation, that he still couldn't remember whether he'd promised Thursday or Friday dinner. It wasn't him.

Strauss worried about Halverson's shaped columns and whether oversight would ask for the raw set after all. It wasn't enough, and it wasn't this.

Ordinary thoughts kept surfacing. Ordinary thoughts kept clearing people by accident. It should have made the room feel safer. It didn't. It only proved how many covers ordinary life could wear.

The quartet brightened. Glasses clinked. Somebody laughed too loudly near the terrace doors. Somewhere in the middle of all that, a usable line into WISE either existed or had been believed into operational reality by people with schedules and windows and handoffs.

That was enough to move on.

She set the empty glass on a passing tray and started assembling the next three days in her head. Tomorrow she would pull review calendars and coverage changes. After that she would map who in WISE could see both the shifts and Damian's lane. Then she would work through everyone from tonight who had business caring when those windows closed.

She'd do it quietly. She'd do it alone until she had something that could survive a question.

And if that meant Damian felt her watching him more closely, or Yuri decided she attended one function too many, or Sylvia asked why she suddenly cared so much about access rosters, she'd deal with that when it came.

For now she went to find her coat with the phrase still lodged under her ribs and the hunt finally shaped enough to start.
state_deltas_required: assassination attempt on Damian; survival followed by proximity and emotional vulnerability; the recognition nearly completes
Physical danger survived together. The emotional boundary is crossed — a kiss, an intimacy neither can undo. Anya's telepathy during intimacy creates a moral problem. The recognition is now imminent.
